_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**1. Ocean Carrier CII Monitoring Infrastructure**
Scope: All ocean carriers contracted by the workspace on primary trade lanes (Americas–Europe, Americas–Asia, Europe–Asia). The workspace currently lacks a systematic process for ingesting annual CII ratings (A–E) into carrier qualification decisions. Gap from baseline: manual, ad hoc. Required build: a carrier scorecard that incorporates CII rating as a weighted criterion alongside transit time, capacity reliability, and pricing. CII ratings are publicly available via IMO GISIS / flag state databases and third-party maritime data providers. Deadline: implement before next ocean carrier RFP cycle (Q3 2026 at latest, given MEPC 85 adoption vote).

**2. Well-to-Wake Emissions Data Collection System**
Scope: All ocean shipments. The workspace must be capable of producing per-shipment WTW GHG emission estimates to serve client Scope 3 reporting needs and to position itself as a compliant, data-transparent forwarder as the NZF enters force. Gap from baseline: most TMS/freight audit platforms use tank-to-wake (TTW) emission factors derived from GLEC Framework or IMO DCS data; WTW methodology per MEPC.376(80) LCA Guidelines adds well-to-tank factors that vary by fuel type and production pathway. Required build: update emission-factor library in the workspace's carbon accounting or TMS tool to accommodate WTW factors; negotiate data-sharing addenda with top 10 ocean carrier partners to receive voyage-level fuel consumption data. Deadline: begin procurement/build Q3 2026; operational for 2027 annual reporting cycles ahead of NZF entry into force.

**3. Carrier Contract Clause — GHG Surcharge Governance**
Scope: All ocean carrier master service agreements (MSAs) and spot booking terms. As NZF Remedial Unit costs are expected to be passed through as surcharges, the workspace needs contractual protection including: (a) advance notice periods for surcharge introduction (minimum 60 days); (b) methodology transparency requirements (carrier must disclose how per-TEU/per-unit surcharge is calculated from its RU exposure); (c) caps on unilateral surcharge quantum increases between rate cycles. Gap from baseline: current MSAs typically contain generic bunker adjustment factor clauses that do not contemplate GHG compliance surcharges. Required build: engage maritime counsel to draft standard GHG surcharge rider; deploy in all renewals Q3–Q4 2026. Deadline: prior to NZF adoption at MEPC 85 (~October 2026).

**4. Supplier Onboarding — Green Fuel Verification**
Scope: Ocean carriers offering 'green' or 'low-carbon' shipment options marketed to workspace clients (relevant for fine art, luxury goods, and high-value automotive verticals with brand sustainability commitments). The workspace must verify that any green ocean product offered to clients rests on carrier fuel certification that will be recognised under the NZF (IMO-approved Sustainable Fuel Certification Scheme). Gap from baseline: current onboarding does not assess fuel certification validity. Required build: add fuel certification documentation requirement to carrier ESG due-diligence questionnaire; cross-check against MEPC-recognised schemes once NZF implementation guidelines are published (expected post-MEPC 85). Deadline: implement in next annual carrier ESG review cycle.

**5. Staff Training — Regulatory Literacy**
Scope: Ocean freight procurement team, sustainability/ESG team, and client-facing key account managers (especially those serving luxury, automotive, and humanitarian verticals). Training content: IMO GHG Strategy structure; current CII/EEXI regime; NZF GFI mechanics (Tier 1/Tier 2, Remedial Units, Surplus Units); WTW vs. TTW distinction; interaction with EU ETS and FuelEU Maritime for Europe-touching trade lanes. Gap from baseline: most operational staff understand bunker surcharges but lack GFI and WTW literacy needed to explain cost drivers to clients or to negotiate intelligently with carriers. Deadline: deliver initial training module Q3 2026.

*Source: IMO — IMO approves net-zero regulations for global shipping (MEPC 83 outcomes), IMO, April 2025. https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/pressbriefings/pages/imo-approves-netzero-regulations.aspx; IMO Net-Zero Framework FAQs, IMO, 2025. https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/hottopics/pages/faqs-the-imo-net-zero-framework.aspx*
